```json
{
  "fullTexts": [
    {
      "fetchError": null,

      "sourceUrl": "https://www.govinfo.gov/bulkdata/BILLS/119/1/s/BILLS-119s2874is.xml",
      "text": "II\n119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nS. 2874\nIN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES\nSeptember 18 (legislative day, September 16), 2025 Mr. Husted (for himself and Mr. Warnock ) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance\nA BILL\nTo provide for the reliquidation of certain entries of golf cart tires.\n#### 1. Reliquidation of certain entries of golf cart tires\n##### (a) Findings\nCongress makes the following findings:\n**(1)**\nU.S. Customs and Border Protection determined in New York Ruling Letter number NY N278164, dated September 1, 2016, that model K389 Hole-N-One golf cart tires are classified under subheading 4011.69.00 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States (as in effect on September 1, 2016) at a duty rate of free .\n**(2)**\nU.S. Customs and Border Protection affirmed that ruling letter in Headquarters Ruling Letter number HQ H285180, dated May 22, 2017.\n##### (b) Sense of Congress\nIt is the sense of Congress that, in conformity with the ruling letters specified in subsection (a), U.S. Customs and Border Protection should be authorized to reliquidate duty-paid entries of K389 Hole-N-One golf cart tires in the case in which liquidation of such entries occurred outside the timeframe specified in section 514 of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( 19 U.S.C. 1514 ).\n##### (c) Reliquidation and refund of duties\nNotwithstanding section 514 of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( 19 U.S.C. 1514 ) or any other provision of law, U.S. Customs and Border Protection shall, not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act\u2014\n**(1)**\nreliquidate each entry of K389 Hole-N-One golf cart tires specified in subsection (d) at the rate of duty applicable to such tires under subheading 4011.69.00 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States on the date of entry; and\n**(2)**\nrefund with interest any duties previously paid with respect to the entry.\n##### (d) Affected entries\nThe entries specified in this subsection are the following:\nMonitor Manufacturing Co. Importer No. 31\u2013162600400\nEntry Number\nEntry Date\nPort of Entry\n231\u20138143412\u20130\n3/5/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138144604\u20131\n3/15/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138145616\u20134\n3/22/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138146206\u20133\n3/26/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138146208\u20139\n4/2/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138148374\u20137\n4/2/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138146527\u20132\n4/2/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138146433\u20133\n4/7/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138151666\u20130\n5/28/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138151979\u20137\n6/1/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138152880\u20136\n6/8/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138153048\u20139\n6/10/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138154434\u20130\n6/22/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138153745\u20130\n6/14/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138153055\u20134\n6/15/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138153752\u20136\n6/23/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138154279\u20139\n6/29/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138154575\u20130\n7/9/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138155290\u20135\n7/21/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138157065\u20139\n7/21/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138155743\u20133\n7/28/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138157062\u20136\n8/3/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138158119\u20133\n8/8/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138158690\u20133\n8/17/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138159396\u20136\n8/24/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138160016\u20137\n8/30/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000321\u20138\n8/31/2010\nAtlanta\n231\u20138160830\u20131\n9/12/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138160014\u20132\n9/13/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138161960\u20135\n9/18/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138161372\u20133\n9/20/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138192260\u20139\n9/20/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138162794\u20137\n9/26/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138164178\u20131\n9/27/2009\nAtlanta\n231\u20138162746\u20137\n9/28/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000047\u20139\n10/5/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000048\u20137\n10/9/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000053\u20137\n10/15/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000057\u20138\n10/17/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000058\u20136\n10/17/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000061\u20130\n10/20/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000062\u20138\n10/25/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000064\u20134\n10/25/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000068\u20135\n10/25/2009\nAtlanta\nE6U\u20130000077 A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is \"to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives\". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.",
```

```json
          "action-date": "2025-09-18",
          "action-desc": "Introduced in Senate",
          "summary-text": "<p>This bill provides for the reliquidation of certain golf cart tires imported into the United States during specified time periods. U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) defines <em>liquidation</em> as the final computation or ascertainment of duties on entries for consumption or drawback entries.</p><p>Within 90 days of this bill's enactment, CBP must</p><ul><li>reliquidate each entry of K389 Hole-N-One golf cart tires at the rate of duty applicable to such tires under subheading 4011.69.00 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States (the general rate of duty for this subheading is free) on the date of entry, and</li><li>refund with interest any duties previously paid with respect to the entry.</li></ul>"
        },
        "title": "A bill to provide for the reliquidation of certain entries of golf cart tires."
      }
    },
```
